{{Quote|I knew this relic was important.|[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] officer [[Naren Bluuis]]|<ref name="Relic" />}}
The '''Kashi Mer talisman''' was a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] artifact from the ancient [[Kashi Mer Dynasty|Kashi Mer monarchy]]. It was used by [[Arden Lyn]], the consort of [[Xendor]], to preserve her body for thousands of years before she was restored. It was later sought after by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Durrei]], in competition with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==Description==
The talisman was a prism-shaped piece of gray rock, measuring approximately 20 by 10 centimeters. The artifact was endowed with [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] powers and radiated an aura of evil. The rock was cool to the touch, and conveyed a sense of power to its holder, endowing them with the knowledge of the relic's full potential. This was believed to be the ability to tap directly into the dark side.<ref name="Relic" />

==History==
===Kashi Mer dynasty===
[[File:ArdenLyn_SWGamer.jpg|thumb|left|Arden Lyn was resurrected by the power of the talisman.]]
The talisman belonged to the [[Kashi Mer Dynasty|Kashi Mer dynasty]] for many years, becoming something of a family heirloom. However, the artifact was stolen by [[Reda Jalooz]], a young student of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Jalooz had discovered a "dark secret" about the talisman that motivated her to steal it. Having experimented with the item over a number of months, she became convinced of her wrong-doing, and was able to conquer the power of the talisman. She voluntarily returned to [[Kashi]]—though without the relic—and begged forgiveness of its people. Her contrition brought no peace to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], though, as the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] [[Star/Legends|sun]] went [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] shortly after her arrival, destroying all life on the planet and obliterating its thousands–year old culture.<ref name="Relic" />

The talisman was then somehow obtained by the Kashi Mer exile, [[Xendor]] and his consort [[Arden Lyn]]. Xendor was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knight]] who left the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Order]], taking a number of Jedi with him to form the [[Legions of Lettow]], in what became known as the [[First Great Schism]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Awdrysta Pina]] killed Xendor during the [[Battle of Columus]] and later [[Duel on Irkalla|cornered]] Arden Lyn on [[Irkalla]]<ref name="TGW" /> in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> There, Lyn unleashed the relic's dark powers to kill Pina by shattering his sword and driving the fragments into his body. However, Pina, in his dying moments, used the Jedi art of [[Morichro/Legends|morichro]] to arrest Lyn's bodily functions, seemingly killing her. In actuality, Lyn, a member of the [[Followers of Palawa]], used their Force-trance ability to create a form of suspended animation—aided by the talisman—to stave off death.<ref name="TGW" />

===Re-emergence===
The relic fell nearby, where it was to lie undiscovered for some 250 centuries, until the time of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. The [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]], [[Ameesa Darys]], and [[Grand Inquisitor/Legends|Grand Inquisitor]] [[Laddinare Torbin]] discovered the planet, and found the relic<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" /> in a [[Arden Lyn's tomb|tomb]]<ref name="TGW" /> lying near a desiccated, but well preserved, corpse—that of Arden Lyn. They inadvertently brought the relic close to the body, causing it to restore life to Lyn with a searing scream. Lyn believed them responsible for the death of her lover, Xendor, and set out to kill them.<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" />

She severely injured Tremayne and killed Darys before Torbin cut off her arm and apprehended her. She was taken to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who promised to revive Xendor from [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] in exchange for her becoming one of his [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]].<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" />

While Lyn served the Emperor for many years, she soon realized he had no intention of keeping his promise, something that should have been capable through the power of the talisman. As a result, Lyn allied with [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] and attempted to kidnap the Emperor in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Despite the plot's initial success, it was ultimately foiled by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]. Fearing reprisals, Lyn fled the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], pursued by a feuding Tremayne. She began searching for the talisman, in the hopes of resurrecting Xendor herself.<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" />

[[File:Durrei.jpg|thumb|right|The Dark Jedi Durrei sought the relic.]]

===Conflict===
{{Quote|Do not let the artifact fall into their hands. Take it. Use what they desire most against them.|Durrei strives to keep the relic for himself|<ref name="Relic" />}}
The Emperor was killed soon after at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and the talisman disappeared. It resurfaced in the [[Corva sector/Legends|Corva sector]], [[9 ABY/Legends|five years later]]. The [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Mahk'khar]] obtained the relic on the planet [[Tuulab]]. He planned to trade it with the Dark Jedi, [[Durrei]] who was working for an Imperial faction in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. The [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] had also heard of the talisman and dispatched a team to locate and retrieve the artifact. The team attacked Durrei and Mahk'khar during their exchange on the planet [[Jandoon]], but were unsuccessful in preventing Durrei from making off with the artifact. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] team continued to pursue Durrei.<ref name="Relic" />

Arden Lyn, however, was never able to recover the talisman, as one year later, she was killed by the resurrected Emperor for her betrayal.<ref name="jvs">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
This talisman first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' article "Relic," published on [[May 1]], [[1995]].<ref name="Relic" /> Its only other appearance has been in the [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]] article "The Emperor's Pawns," released on May 1, [[2001]] and written by [[Abel G. Peña]].<ref name="The Emperor's Pawns" />

The only published image of the talisman appears to differ from the description of the artifact. The image is light in coloration, rather than dark or gray and is curved rather than being a prism.<ref name="Relic" />
